description: Adopted whooo's lib+bin module-boundary pattern (P-203) — proven across one prior experiment, low risk to reuse
Comparator-as-data pattern (COMPARATORS object map) instead of switch statement. Extensibility win: add 'random' or 'reverse-natural' modes by adding map entries, not modifying control flow.

NaN-numeric sorts to end via Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY in parseNumeric. Deterministic and testable. Alternative (NaN at start, or filter out non-numeric) was considered and rejected — silent filtering would surprise users.

Synchronous stdin via fs.readFileSync(0). Async stdin (chunks via stream) was considered but rejected — sync matches CLI shape, eliminates buffering bugs, and node:test spawnSync input: option drives it cleanly.
P-204 (assert SHAPE not VALUE) applied to all CLI output tests — regex `/\r?\n/` makes tests pass identically on Windows and Unix without forks
P-201 (real-world fixture) applied via PAN CHANGELOG headers — the dogfood-style test caught nothing new this run, but proves the principle scales
Trailing-newline-on-output convention is enforced silently in joinLines; tests check it but the user-facing docs (--help) don't mention it. Could surprise scripts that count bytes.
